# Example test file to demonstrate dependency graph tools

# Create test data
mkdir -p testdata/demo
exec sh -c 'echo "exec echo test" > testdata/demo/test1.txt'
exec sh -c 'echo "exec mcpdiff file1 file2" >> testdata/demo/test1.txt'
exec sh -c 'echo "mcp-server-start demo go run server.go" >> testdata/demo/test1.txt'

exec sh -c 'echo "exec echo test2" > testdata/demo/test2.txt'
exec sh -c 'echo "exec grep pattern file" >> testdata/demo/test2.txt'
exec sh -c 'echo "exec mcpdiff file3 file4" >> testdata/demo/test2.txt'

# Generate callgraph
exec testgraph -format json testdata/demo > callgraph.json
exec cat callgraph.json
stdout '"nodes"'
stdout '"edges"'

# Transform to dependency graph
exec depgraph -input callgraph.json -format json > depgraph.json
exec cat depgraph.json
stdout '"nodesByType"'
stdout '"statistics"'

# Generate different views
exec depgraph -input callgraph.json -direction program-to-test -format digraph > reverse.digraph
exec cat reverse.digraph
stdout 'echo test1.txt'
stdout 'mcpdiff test1.txt'

# Generate transitive closure
exec depgraph -input callgraph.json -type transitive -format dot > transitive.dot
exec cat transitive.dot
stdout 'digraph dependencies'

# Generate adjacency matrix
exec depgraph -input callgraph.json -format matrix > matrix.txt
exec cat matrix.txt
stdout 'test1.txt'
stdout 'test2.txt'

# Show grouped visualization
exec depgraph -input callgraph.json -format dot -group > grouped.dot
exec cat grouped.dot
stdout 'subgraph cluster_test'
stdout 'subgraph cluster_program'

echo "Dependency graph tools working correctly"
